title: draba arctica subsp. groenlandica
intro: Draba arctica subsp. groenlandica, also known as the Greenland whitlowgrass, is a plant species native to the Arctic regions, specifically found in Greenland and other areas of the Arctic tundra. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating colds, coughs, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other beneficial compounds that contribute to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects and provide relief from various health conditions.
